elements.name: Title
texts: "One of the loneliest places on earth"

elements.name: Description
texts: "[Lough Derg] is one of the loneliest places on earth. Its boulder-strewn shores, thirteen miles in circumference, seem to have retained all their original wildness..."

elements.name: Source
texts: Curtayne, Alice, Lough Derg: St. Patrick’s Purgatory, p. 1

elements.name: Publisher
texts: Burns Oats and Washbourn, Ltd., London and Dublin

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts: "[Lough Derg] is one of the loneliest places on earth. Its boulder-strewn shores, thirteen miles in circumference, seem to have retained all their original wildness. On some of the southern hill-slopes in recent years, the afforestation authorities have planted several thousand larch, spruce and fir-trees, whose green spires are now showing above the nursing knee-high heather. But there is little other cultivation to be seen."
